title: Locksmith Service VA
description:
Job Summary:

We are looking for a dependable and clear communicator to join our remote team as a Locksmith Sales Support VA. In this role, you will handle inbound customer calls, converting inquiries into job orders while addressing customer needs professionally and efficiently. 



Your key duties will include providing accurate information, offering suitable solutions, and scheduling appointments—all while maintaining a friendly, customer-centric approach. A background in locksmith services, sales, and working in a fast-paced, reactive environment is preferred.



Key Responsibilities:

-Respond to inbound calls regarding locksmith services, delivering precise and timely information to customers.

-Convert customer inquiries into sales by recommending suitable solutions and booking service appointments.

-Utilize CRM tools to log interactions, update customer details, and track lead progress.

-Follow call-handling protocols and use locksmith-specific terminology.

-Manage job orders, including pricing, scheduling, and coordinating technician dispatch.

-Communicate with professionalism and empathy, ensuring a positive customer experience.

-Collaborate with the dispatch and internal teams to resolve issues and ensure prompt service delivery.

-Ensure all customer queries are handled within defined timeframes, meeting service level agreements (SLAs).



Mandatory Skills:

-2+ years of experience in dispatch or locksmith services.

-Experience working in a reactive environment (e.g., responding to urgent, time-sensitive service requests).

-Excellent spoken English with a neutral accent.

-Proven sales experience, particularly in converting inquiries into bookings.

-Strong communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to upsell services when appropriate.

-Familiarity with CRM tools and call management software.

-Ability to remain calm and efficient in a high-call volume environment.

-Reliable internet connection and a quiet, distraction-free workspace.

-Strong attention to detail and adherence to structured processes.



Preferred Skills:

-Prior experience in a call center, dispatch, or customer support role.

-Familiarity with lead tracking or ticketing systems.

-Quick ability to learn new technical or service-related terms.

-Experience working remotely with minimal supervision.

-Knowledge of tools like Syncrorma PBX or custom CRMs (a plus, not required).



Why Join Us:

This fully remote position offers flexibility, structured support, and the chance to work in a performance-driven environment. You'll be part of a team that values clear communication, continuous improvement, and professional growth. With warm leads provided and no cold calling, your focus will be on converting calls into job orders and delivering exceptional service in an organized and supportive environment.

title: Afterhours Engineer - NOC/SOC/Support
description:
Please apply for this job here: 



JOB SUMMARY

The Afterhours Engineer - NOC\SOC\Support is important in the Service Delivery division at CTS. Operating as the primary guardian of our clients' infrastructure during off-peak hours, this role combines Network Operations (NOC), Security Operations (SOC), and high-level VIP support.



Responsible for delivering impeccable maintenance, security monitoring, and "white-glove" support, this engineer must exhibit a sense of ownership over various systems. You will be responsible for triaging security alerts, executing i
 response playbooks, and ensuring VIP requests are resolved immediately, ensuring our clients’ environments are stable and secure before the morning shift arrives.



This is an individual contributor - technical position.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Service Delivery: Respond to and resolve service i
 following existing procedures and Service Level Agreements (SLAs). Track time, communications, and work output via ConnectWise Manage PSA.

Network Operations (NOC) Monitoring & Remediation: Remediate alerts from monitoring tools generated from firewalls, switches, access points, physical and virtual workstations, servers, and storage devices. Configure monitoring of client infrastructure using PRTG, Kaseya VSA, and ConnectWise Manage.

Security & I
 Response (SOC): Act as the first line of defense for security alerts. Triage potential threats (e.g., suspicious logins, EDR alerts, phishing reports) using established I
 Response playbooks. Isolate infected endpoints to prevent lateral movement and document forensic details.

VIP & Helpdesk Support: Provide primary "white-glove" remote support for VIP clients (CEOs/Executives) experiencing after-hours technical issues. Address all phone calls and urgent tickets outside of regular business hours with a focus on rapid resolution.

Cloud Administration: Administer and troubleshoot Microsoft 365 (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint) and Google Workspace environments to resolve urgent user access or configuration issues.

Maintenance & Automation: Administer and perform the work for monthly, quarterly, bi-annual, and yearly maintenance plans. Assist higher-level Engineers to automate recurring requests using Ninja RMM, ConnectWise Manage, PRTG, and PowerShell.

Backup Management: Monitor and remediate all CTS-provided client backup solutions: Acronis On-Prem, Acronis Cloud, and AFI.

Documentation: Create and maintain information and documentation in ConnectWise Manage and ITGlue to ensure a smooth handoff to the daytime engineering team.



REQUIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

2+ years of experience in an MSP (Managed Service Provider) environment, specifically in a NOC or an L2/L3 escalation role.

Cybersecurity Fundamentals: Strong understanding of cybersecurity principles, threat landscapes (ransomware, phishing), and experience executing I
 Response playbooks.

Cloud Platforms: Advanced administration skills in Microsoft 365 (User management, Intune, MFA troubleshooting) and Google Workspace.

Networking: Experience with networking protocols: TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, VPN, IPSEC, VoIP, and QoS. Experience in WAN/LAN networking, including routing, switching, security, and load balancing.

OS Support: Experience with Microsoft Windows Server, Windows Desktop, and Apple Ecosystem.

WLAN & Hardware: Experience in WLAN technologies (Meraki, UniFi, Cisco) and Network Attached Storage (NAS).

VoIP: Experience with Grand Stream or other Asterisk-based PBX systems.

Soft Skills: Exceptional written and oral communication skills with the ability to de-escalate stressful situations for VIP clients.

Work Ethic: Self-motivated, with excellent time management skills and the ability to work independently without supervision during overnight hours.



PREFERRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

MSP experience.

Knowledge of scripting/programming language, including, but not limited to, PowerShell, BASH, and/or VBScript

Knowledge of network vendors: SonicWALL, Juniper MIST, Meraki, and Unifi

Knowledge of security tools: EDR/XDR,
 Filtering, Security Awareness, and Cloud Backups

Experience with MSP-related tools: ConnectWise Manage, Ninja RMM, PRTG, and ITGlue



WORK LOCATION & SCHEDULE

This is a full-time remote position for candidates located in the Philippines

The hours for this position are 6pm-6am EST, 4x/week including weekends (on a rotation basis)

COMPENSATION



The salary range for this role is $1600 - $2500/monthly



BENEFITS

Paid Time Off

Floating Holidays



This is an independent contractor role.

title: Property Management Operations Specialist
description:
EXPERIENCED PROPERTY MANAGEMENT VA (BUILDIUM REQUIRED – READ BEFORE APPLYING)

Pacific Homefront Property Management (California, USA)



We are hiring ONE experienced Virtual Assistant with real, hands-on Buildium experience to support and help improve our property management operations. MUST also have bookkeeping experience.



IMPORTANT:

If you do NOT have actual experience using Buildium in a property management role, do not apply.



This is not a beginner role. We are looking for someone who can operate independently, solve problems, and help improve systems.



RESPONSIBILITIES



Manage property operations inside Buildium



Tenant communication, rent tracking, and delinquency follow-ups



Maintenance coordination and vendor communication



Lease tracking, move-ins and move-outs



Identify issues and propose improvements to workflows



Support owner communication and reporting



Help maintain and improve SOPs and systems



REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)



Minimum 3+ years U.S. property management experience



Hands-on Buildium experience (not just “familiar with it”)



Strong understanding of rent collection, maintenance coordination, and leasing processes



Ability to work without constant supervision



Strong English communication (written and verbal)



Stable internet and professional work setup



DO NOT APPLY IF



You have never used Buildium in real work



You need step-by-step instructions for everything



You are applying to multiple jobs without reading this post



WHAT WE VALUE



Initiative (you identify problems before we do)



Accountability (you own results, not just tasks)



Organization and attention to detail



Ability to think and make decisions independently



GROWTH OPPORTUNITY

This role can grow into a Team Lead or Operations Manager position based on performance.



HOW TO APPLY (MANDATORY)

To be considered, your application MUST include:



The word HOMEFRONT at the top of your application



A brief summary of your Buildium experience (specific tasks you performed)



Your years of U.S. property management experience



A short example of a time you solved a problem without being told



Your current number of clients or jobs



Applications that do not follow ALL instructions will be ignored.



COMPENSATION

Based on experience and skill level, with opportunity for long-term growth.



If you are looking for a serious role where you can grow, take ownership, and help build a strong operation, we want to hear from you.
